title: Synthetic Genetic Network for Biochemical Noise and Pattern Formation
summary: Modeling how stochastic gene-expression fluctuations and local coupling can generate switching fronts, domain formation, and spatially heterogeneous behavior in engineered biological systems.

title: Mathematical and Computational Modeling of an Optogenetic Circuit
summary: Building quantitative models for light-responsive genetic regulation, with emphasis on parameter inference, controllability, and experimentally interpretable dynamics.

title: Lattice-Based Self-Activating Toggle-Switch Model
summary: Exploring how local interactions, stochastic switching, and self-activation can produce collective spatial order in populations of cells carrying toggle-like circuits.

title: Ising-Like Spatial Patterns in Bacterial Populations
summary: Using statistical-physics ideas to interpret emergent domains, switching boundaries, and order-disorder transitions in communicating biological populations.

title: Python Microscopy Image-Processing Pipelines
summary: Developing reproducible analysis workflows for microscopy experiments, including segmentation, measurement extraction, quality control, and downstream data integration.

title: Open-Source Biotechnology and Molecular Biology Education
summary: Creating public-facing educational materials that connect molecular biology practice, computational modeling, and accessible scientific communication.
